caveats: a: No public wage-weighted task inventory specific to oilseed processing was found.
a: Food-processing occupation data are broader than oilseed crushing and may miss commodity-origination and laboratory roles.
rho: This is bounded implementation judgment, not an observed adoption rate.
rho: Mechanical-press specialty plants may have different economics and automation maturity from large solvent-extraction facilities.
e: The frozen estimate of 28 LMM firms is margin-bridged, not observed.
e: A public processor reports that four companies control nearly 85% of U.S. soybean processing, which may mean the size-class bridge materially overstates independent targets.
s5: Gallup covers all employer businesses, not 311224 or the LMM EBITDA band.
s5: Planned sales and gifts are not completed qualifying control transfers.
q: Public-company segments include origination, trading, refining, and other activities beyond the frozen lens.
q: No source directly measures the retained share of an AI-enabled benefit.
d5: The evidence is soybean-heavy while NAICS 311224 includes other oilseeds and tree nuts.
d5: Biofuel-driven demand is policy-sensitive and added capacity can pressure margins even as quantity grows.
o: This is a structural estimate, not an observed five-year operator-retention share.
o: Demand volume is handled in d5 rather than counted again here.

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Recurring B2B sales of commodity or specification-based meal, oil, hulls, protein, and co-products, with economics driven by throughput, local origination and logistics, crush spreads, quality, and product mix.
lens.excluded_activities: Captive internal plants, stand-alone refining or blending of purchased oils, wet corn milling, farming, commodity trading without processing assets, shell entities, and non-control financing vehicles.
lens.included_activities: Independent merchant processors that crush soybeans, cottonseed, canola, sunflower seed, peanuts, flaxseed, or other oilseeds and tree nuts into oils, meal, hulls, cakes, protein concentrates, and related co-products for repeat sale to external feed, food, biofuel, and industrial customers.

a.bridge: BLS food-processing equipment work proxies the 311224 occupation mix. I map process monitoring, production records, quality review, maintenance triage, scheduling, origination analytics, logistics, and administrative work to AI exposure while excluding most receiving, cleaning, crushing, extraction, maintenance, loading, and safety work. This likely overstates wage-weighted opportunity because the frozen compensation-to-receipts ratio is only 1.32% and modern high-throughput plants already rely on automated equipment.
caveats: No public wage-weighted task inventory specific to oilseed processing was found.
Food-processing occupation data are broader than oilseed crushing and may miss commodity-origination and laboratory roles.
a.change_evidence: A 311224-specific OEWS staffing and wage table plus time-and-motion studies at solvent-extraction and mechanical-press plants that identify already automated tasks and remaining manual hours by function.
a.rationale: AI can assist information-heavy work around a crush plant, especially exception detection, planning, records, quality, procurement, and transport. Core conversion is physical, continuous, asset-intensive, and safety-critical; direct labor is already a very small share of receipts.

d5.bridge: USDA forecast 2025/26 U.S. soybean crush up nearly 3% to a record 2.49 billion bushels, with crush reaching 57% of soybean production, driven by meal and biomass-diesel oil demand; EIA forecast 2026 renewable-diesel output up 24% and biodiesel up 41%. I temper those near-term growth rates into a five-year constant-quality demand range because new capacity, biofuel policy, feed cycles, exports, and alternative oils create uncertainty. The sources may overstate durable growth if mandates weaken or competing feedstocks gain share.
caveats: The evidence is soybean-heavy while NAICS 311224 includes other oilseeds and tree nuts.
Biofuel-driven demand is policy-sensitive and added capacity can pressure margins even as quantity grows.
d5.change_evidence: USDA Oil Crops balance sheets through 2031, EPA renewable-volume obligations, EIA biofuel feedstock forecasts, announced crush capacity and utilization, soybean-meal export and livestock-feed forecasts, and project cancellation data.
d5.rationale: Domestic crush is expanding from record levels as livestock feed and biomass-based diesel increase demand for meal and oil. The low case still allows modest growth, while the upper range requires sustained biofuel policy and absorption of new capacity without major substitution or export weakness.

caveats: The frozen estimate of 28 LMM firms is margin-bridged, not observed.
A public processor reports that four companies control nearly 85% of U.S. soybean processing, which may mean the size-class bridge materially overstates independent targets.
e.change_evidence: A full 311224 establishment-to-firm map with ownership, EBITDA, merchant versus captive output, capacity, customer and supplier concentration, cooperative or JV restrictions, shared assets, and environmental liabilities.
e.rationale: A standalone merchant processor with separable assets, recurring external customers, compliant operations, and defensible local origination can qualify. Eligibility is reduced by concentrated strategic ownership, vertical integration, plants above the EBITDA band, cooperatives or joint ventures with transfer restrictions, shared infrastructure, commodity-only economics, and environmental or process-safety liabilities.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 311224 ($862,675,026) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 311224 ($69,641,445,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

n.detail: margin 0.1525 (margins.json[31]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Food Processing EBITDA/Sales 15.25% (dominant subsector of NAICS 31)); revenue/employee $7,590,348 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 311224); class <5: 30 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,341,867 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $509,635 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 15 firms, avg revenue/firm $8,201,800 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,250,774 -> IN $1-10M band; class 10-19: 13 firms, avg revenue/firm $18,397,923 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $2,805,683 -> IN $1-10M band; class 20-99: 16 firms, avg revenue/firm $107,681,250 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $16,421,391 -> out of $1-10M band; class 100-499: 13 firms, avg revenue/firm $384,939,769 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $58,703,315 -> out of $1-10M band; class 500+: 15 firms, avg revenue/firm $4,163,458,133 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $634,927,365 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 28

caveats: This is a structural estimate, not an observed five-year operator-retention share.
Demand volume is handled in d5 rather than counted again here.
o.change_evidence: Customer make-versus-buy surveys, announced captive capacity, import penetration, alternative-oil and meal substitution studies, and qualification requirements for food, feed, and biofuel buyers.
o.rationale: Software cannot replace the physical crushing, extraction, separation, testing, storage, and shipment needed to turn oilseeds into meal, oil, hulls, and proteins. AI can reduce labor intensity but not remove accountable plant operation. Residual displacement reflects imports, end-user self-supply, vertical integration, alternative feedstocks, or customer relocation.

q.bridge: ADM reports oilseeds as an agricultural commodity business where selling prices move with raw-material prices, while South Dakota Soybean Processors describes cyclical variable crush margins and an industry competing on efficient low-cost operation. I map these public-company economics to LMM benefit retention: general cost savings are competed into crush bids and product prices, while localized reliability, yield, energy, and specialty-product gains can persist longer. The proxy may overstate LMM retention because smaller firms lack scale, or understate it for differentiated non-GMO and specialty processors.
caveats: Public-company segments include origination, trading, refining, and other activities beyond the frozen lens.
No source directly measures the retained share of an AI-enabled benefit.
q.change_evidence: Target purchase and sales contracts, bid histories, hedge books, invoice cohorts, crush-spread realization, product-level margins, customer switching, and price response following documented productivity improvements.
q.rationale: Meal and oil are tradable, substitutable commodities and raw-material procurement is competitive. Buyers and soybean suppliers can capture much of a broad efficiency gain through price and crush-spread adjustment; retention is strongest where the operator has logistics advantages, constrained local capacity, specialty specifications, or proprietary co-product value.

caveats: This is bounded implementation judgment, not an observed adoption rate.
Mechanical-press specialty plants may have different economics and automation maturity from large solvent-extraction facilities.
rho.change_evidence: Audited plant pilots reporting baseline labor, deployment cost, model error, downtime, yield, energy, quality deviations, safety review, and sustained headcount or hiring effects for at least twelve months.
rho.rationale: Decision support, document automation, predictive-maintenance triage, inspection, production scheduling, freight planning, and commodity analytics can be layered onto existing digital operations within five years. Process safety, food and feed compliance, cyber controls, legacy integration, and the high cost of outages constrain unattended implementation, so augmentation and avoided hiring dominate.

s5.bridge: Gallup found 22% of U.S. employer-business owners planned a sale or ownership transfer within five years. I reduce the center for a mature, consolidated, vertically integrated processing sector where corporate, cooperative, joint-venture, or family ownership and asset interdependence can make transfers non-qualifying. The broad survey can overstate qualifying transfers but excludes unplanned strategic deals.
caveats: Gallup covers all employer businesses, not 311224 or the LMM EBITDA band.
Planned sales and gifts are not completed qualifying control transfers.
s5.change_evidence: PitchBook, Capital IQ, GF Data, SEC filings, and state-registry review of every U.S. oilseed-processor control transfer since 2016, combined with owner-age and succession interviews for all independent operators.
s5.rationale: The five-year employer-business plan rate is the best broad anchor found, but concentrated ownership and highly specific plant assets lower the probability that an eligible firm produces an external, controlling, operationally transferable deal.

principal_driver: Rising meal and biomass-based-diesel feedstock demand supports growing physical throughput.
principal_weakness: Very low labor intensity and commodity margin transmission sharply limit the economic effect and retention of AI labor gains.

sources.supports: Defines the industry as crushing oilseeds and tree nuts into oilseed oils, cakes, meals, and protein isolates and concentrates.
sources.title: U.S. Census Bureau 2022 NAICS definition: 311224 Soybean and Other Oilseed Processing

sources.supports: Food-processing workers set controls, monitor processes and gauges, record batch data, clean equipment, and check quality; automatic weighing and mixing can reduce worker requirements.
sources.title: BLS Occupational Outlook Handbook: Food Processing Equipment Workers

sources.supports: Forecasts 2025/26 U.S. soybean crush rising nearly 3% to a record 2.49 billion bushels and reaching 57% of soybean production, driven by meal and biomass-diesel oil demand.
sources.title: USDA ERS: More soybeans being processed in United States to meet rising demand for soybean meal and oil

sources.supports: Documents the mix of control, monitoring, recording, sanitation, equipment tending, and quality duties used as the occupation proxy for implementation constraints.
sources.title: BLS: Food-processing duties, work environment, and automation

sources.supports: A fall-2024 survey found 22% of U.S. employer-business owners planned to sell or transfer ownership within five years and 52.3% of employer firms had owners age 55 or older.
sources.title: Gallup: Most Small-Business Owners Lack a Succession Plan

sources.supports: Describes oilseed crushing outputs and customers, agricultural commodity price transmission, capacity operation, margin compression, operational risks, and extensive hedging of anticipated soybean crush.
sources.title: Archer-Daniels-Midland Company 2025 Form 10-K

sources.supports: Reports cyclical variable crush margins, meal/oil uses, industry structure of about 20 companies and 70 facilities, and nearly 85% control by four processors; also documents plant energy and regulatory requirements.
sources.title: South Dakota Soybean Processors 2025 Form 10-K

sources.supports: Forecasts 2026 renewable-diesel production up 24% and biodiesel production up 41% from 2025, with further increases in 2027.
sources.title: U.S. EIA: Higher blending targets drive RIN prices close to record highs

title: Soybean and Other Oilseed Processing
